Title: The versatility of plant organic acid metabolism in leaves is underpinned by mitochondrial malate–citrate exchange

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mitochondrial matrix; extra-mitochondrial space/leaf nighttime citrate pool
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates mitochondrial malate import coupled to citrate export; loss of DIC2 reduces citrate export, alters organic acid homeostasis, increases diversion to amino acid synthesis, elevates dark respiration, and retards vegetative growth.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression increases during the night, peaks near the end of the dark period, and is lowest in the light.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Short-day growth; light-to-dark transition; nighttime dark period; prolonged darkness/starvation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIC2 knockout dic2-1 is a null mutant with reduced root and rosette growth, rescued by genomic or 35S-driven DIC2 complementation. 2. Isolated dic2-1 mitochondria show strongly reduced non-energized malate uptake, increased citrate retention, and reduced citrate export upon malate feeding. 3. Proteoliposome assays show DIC2 preferentially exchanges malate against citrate with high affinity. 4. In leaves, loss of DIC2 causes elevated dark respiration, transient NAD redox imbalance at dark shift, reduced flux toward citrate, and diversion of carbon into amino acid synthesis. 5. dic2-1 is hypersensitive to prolonged darkness and accumulates malate, citrate, and 2-oxoglutarate under starvation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis; complementation with native promoter and 35S overexpression; mitochondrial uptake/export assays; proteoliposome transport assay in yeast microsomes; metabolite profiling; 13C-glucose tracing; 14C-malate respiration assay; NAD redox biosensor analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes stunted vegetative growth, higher night respiration, faster sucrose depletion, nighttime starvation signatures, and accelerated dark-induced senescence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DIC1 and DIC3 overexpression could not complement dic2-1 in planta, indicating nonredundant in vivo transport specificity despite sequence similarity. The paper supports DIC2 as a specialist transporter rather than a broad generalist.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize nighttime organic acid homeostasis, respiration efficiency, and stress/starvation resilience, but manipulation may affect growth-redox balance and carbon allocation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-SRM-MS, GC-MS, Q-TOF 13C tracing); targeted proteomics (LC-MRM-MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DIC2 promoter; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter
